glossary.definition: The process of matching and confirming that financial transactions recorded in the core ledger correspond exactly to payment confirmations received from the payments gateway, ensuring no discrepancies remain unresolved.

A single, system-agnostic identifier assigned to a financial transaction at origination and propagated across all downstream systems (ledger, gateway, reporting warehouse) to enable automated matching without manual cross-referencing.

A prioritised list of unmatched or unresolved settlement transactions surfaced to operations analysts for manual investigation. Each item carries contextual enrichment (age, SLA status, counterparty, amounts) to accelerate resolution.
glossary.source_ref: BR-002 — Exception Queue with Automated Routing

glossary.definition: A prioritisation framework using four categories: Must Have, Should Have, Could Have, and Won't Have. Applied in this BRD to classify business requirements by delivery criticality.
Same-day settlement — the target state in which reconciliation is confirmed on the day the transaction occurs, as opposed to the current T+5 average.
